page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288 is a high-density general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. It features 288 vCPUs mapped to 144 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The architecture is x86_64. It is equipped with 1080.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark testing, the c4-standard-288 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. Its multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores rank in the top 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 22997.34 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 427.49 GB/sec. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), contribute to top-tier performance in cryptographic operations, database transactions, and software compilation.

While the server excels in multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and static web serving, it exhibits some performance trade-offs. Specifically, text generation for small LLM models falls into the bottom 25% of benchmarks, though prompt processing for medium 7B models remains in the top 10%. Given its massive core count and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is highly cost-efficient for resource-dense enterprise workloads, large-scale software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and parallelized CPU-bound data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288 is a high-density general-purpose server featuring 288 vCPUs and 1080.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with x86_64 architecture, it provides 144 physical cores and a 504 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmarks, it delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-throughput database operations, such as Redis SET operations. However, text generation for small LLM models is a relative bottleneck. This server is highly suited for large-scale software compilation, enterprise database hosting, and high-volume static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288 is an x86_64 server with 288 vCPUs, 1080 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288 features 288 vCPUs and 1080 GB RAM, offering top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, 288-vCPU x86_64 compute platform engineered for massive multi-threaded workloads, high-throughput database operations, and intensive software compilation.
bullet_points: Powered by a 288-vCPU Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 1080 GB of RAM
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high-speed memory bandwidth
Features Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instruction sets
Optimized for high-throughput database operations and static web serving
Lacks local storage and GPU hardware accelerators
